refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the system is available for productive operation to total scheduled operational time, accounting for planned maintenance, unplanned downtime, and safety-driven shutdowns.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts customer satisfaction and mission success. Refining it requires explicit definition of: (1) the measurement boundary (what constitutes 'available'), (2) the evaluation period (e.g., 30 days, 90 days, 1 year), (3) exclusions (planned maintenance windows, customer-initiated pauses), and (4) the role of safety-driven shutdowns. High safety criticality demands that availability not be achieved at the expense of safety; therefore, safety-driven unavailability is acceptable and must be distinguished from failure-driven unavailability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous telemetry collection of system state (operational, maintenance, shutdown, fault) with timestamped logs. Availability calculated as: (Total Scheduled Time − Unplanned Downtime − Safety Shutdowns) / Total Scheduled Time. Audit trail maintained for all downtime events with root cause classification.
sub_frs: FR-AVAIL-001: The system shall detect and log all unplanned downtime events (failures, faults) with timestamp and root cause.
FR-AVAIL-002: The system shall distinguish between safety-driven unavailability and failure-driven unavailability in availability calculations.
FR-AVAIL-003: The system shall provide real-time availability status and historical availability metrics to the Site Manager and Project Manager.
FR-AVAIL-004: The system shall implement predictive maintenance and fault recovery mechanisms to minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-AVAIL-005: The system shall maintain redundancy or failover capability for critical subsystems to sustain availability targets.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement a centralized availability monitoring and reporting system that continuously tracks system state, logs downtime events, calculates availability metrics in real-time, and provides dashboards to stakeholders.
DP-B: Distribute availability monitoring across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules, with each module reporting its own uptime; aggregate at the NGHE Solution level.

DP-C: Implement redundant subsystems (dual motors, dual control channels, dual safety processors) with automatic failover to eliminate single points of failure and maintain availability during component faults.
DP-D: Implement predictive maintenance scheduling based on usage patterns and component health monitoring, combined with rapid fault detection and automated recovery procedures.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A + DP-D: Implement a centralized availability monitoring and reporting system (DP-A) integrated with predictive maintenance and rapid fault recovery (DP-D). This approach ensures that availability is measured holistically at the system level, while fault prevention and recovery mechanisms actively maintain the target. The combination addresses both measurement (DP-A) and operational excellence (DP-D) required to achieve ≥0.97 availability.

potential_sources: DP-A (monitoring) must receive telemetry from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to assess their availability contributions; this creates a dependency on Telemetry interface.

DP-D (predictive maintenance) must integrate with Vehicle subsystems (motor health, hydraulic pressure, structural integrity) and Safety Services (sensor calibration, safety processor status); this creates dependencies on component-level diagnostics.
DP-A and DP-D are loosely coupled: monitoring informs maintenance scheduling, but maintenance execution is independent of real-time monitoring.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP combination exhibits acceptable coupling. DP-A depends on Telemetry interface (inbound), and DP-D depends on Vehicle diagnostics and Safety Services health status. These are well-defined, unidirectional dependencies that do not create circular constraints. The combination is orthogonal to the functional requirement: availability is achieved through measurement + prevention, not through conflicting mechanisms.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Telemetry interface provides sufficient granularity and frequency to detect downtime events within acceptable latency (e.g., <1 second).
ASSUMPTION: Predictive maintenance algorithms can be trained on historical failure data; if data is sparse, prediction accuracy may be low.
RISK: If monitoring system itself fails, availability metrics become unreliable; mitigation requires redundant monitoring or independent verification.
RISK: Predictive maintenance may introduce false positives (unnecessary maintenance) or false negatives (missed failures); requires continuous tuning.
RISK: Availability target of 0.97 may be unachievable if critical subsystems (e.g., Vehicle propulsion) have inherent reliability <0.97; requires component-level reliability analysis.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-A to explicitly include redundant monitoring channels and independent verification of availability metrics. Refine DP-D to specify the predictive maintenance algorithm (e.g., condition-based monitoring, usage-based scheduling, physics-based degradation models) and the fault recovery procedure (e.g., automatic restart, failover to backup component, graceful degradation).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system integration boundary. It owns the availability KPI and is responsible for orchestrating monitoring, reporting, and maintenance coordination across all subordinate modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). The module includes the centralized availability monitoring system, availability reporting dashboards, and the predictive maintenance scheduler.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Inbound telemetry from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. Provides system state, fault codes, sensor readings, and operational metrics required for availability calculation and predictive maintenance.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Outbound tasking to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules. Includes maintenance scheduling commands (e.g., 'perform predictive maintenance on motor X') and operational directives (e.g., 'reduce duty cycle to extend component life').

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional safety alerts. Inbound: safety-driven shutdown signals from Safety Services. Outbound: availability-related safety notifications (e.g., 'system approaching availability threshold due to component degradation; recommend maintenance window').

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of the availability KPI. It has direct visibility into all subordinate modules via Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert interfaces. Availability monitoring and reporting are system-level concerns that belong at the top level. Predictive maintenance scheduling is also a system-level orchestration function. Minimal diffusion: only the actual fault recovery procedures (e.g., motor restart) are delegated to Vehicle module.

Vehicle module owns propulsion, hydraulics, and structural subsystems, which are critical to availability. However, availability measurement and reporting are system-level concerns that should not be duplicated in Vehicle. Vehicle should own fault detection and local recovery (e.g., motor restart), but not the holistic availability KPI. High diffusion risk if availability monitoring is distributed across Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

Teleoperation module owns remote control and communication subsystems. These are important to availability (network link failures impact availability), but Teleoperation should not own the availability KPI or monitoring system. Teleoperation should report its own uptime via Telemetry, but NGHE Solution aggregates and reports the holistic availability metric.

Safety Services owns safety-critical sensors and shutdown logic. Safety-driven unavailability must be distinguished from failure-driven unavailability, which is a Safety Services responsibility. However, Safety Services should not own the availability KPI or reporting system. Safety Services should report safety status via SafetyAlert, and NGHE Solution incorporates this into availability calculations.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The availability KPI (FR-AVAIL-*) is best contained within the NGHE Solution module because: (1) availability is a system-level property that depends on all subordinate modules; (2) measurement and reporting require a holistic view across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; (3) predictive maintenance scheduling is a system-level orchestration function; (4) NGHE Solution already owns the top-level interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) required to implement the DP. Subordinate modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) contribute to availability through their own reliability and fault recovery, but do not own the availability KPI.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution monitoring system fails, availability metrics become unreliable. Mitigation: implement redundant monitoring or independent verification.

RISK: Predictive maintenance scheduler in NGHE Solution may conflict with Vehicle or Safety Services operational constraints. Mitigation: establish clear prioritization rules (safety > availability > efficiency) and coordination protocols.
RISK: Telemetry interface may not provide sufficient granularity for accurate availability calculation. Mitigation: define telemetry requirements explicitly and validate against DP-A.

RISK: Availability target of 0.97 may require redundancy or failover mechanisms that are not currently in scope. Mitigation: perform component-level reliability analysis to determine if 0.97 is achievable with current architecture.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Explicitly define the telemetry schema required by NGHE Solution for availability monitoring (e.g., system state, fault codes, component health metrics, maintenance history). Establish a clear interface contract between NGHE Solution and subordinate modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) for availability-related data exchange. Define the predictive maintenance algorithm and fault recovery procedures in detail, including decision logic and escalation paths.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (centralized monitoring + predictive maintenance) directly addresses the FR (achieve ≥0.97 availability). The DP is logically sound: measurement enables visibility into availability status, and predictive maintenance + fault recovery minimize unplanned downtime. The DP is implementable with current technology (telemetry systems, condition monitoring, automated recovery). No fundamental mismatch between FR and DP.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK: Predictive maintenance algorithms may require component-specific knowledge (e.g., motor degradation models, hydraulic pressure thresholds) that is distributed across Vehicle and Safety Services modules. Mitigation: establish a clear interface for component health data and maintenance recommendations.

DIFFUSION RISK: Fault recovery procedures (e.g., motor restart, failover to backup component) are owned by Vehicle and Safety Services, not NGHE Solution. Mitigation: define clear ownership boundaries and coordination protocols.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner of the availability KPI and monitoring system. The module has clear ownership of the Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert interfaces required to implement the DP. Subordinate modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) contribute to availability through their own reliability and fault recovery, but do not own the availability KPI. Containment score of 0.95 for NGHE Solution is justified.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-AVAIL-* is feasible through continuous telemetry collection and availability metric calculation. Acceptance criteria: measured availability ≥0.97 over the agreed evaluation period. Verification requires: (1) instrumentation of all system components to report state and faults, (2) continuous logging of availability metrics, (3) independent audit of downtime events and root causes, (4) statistical analysis to confirm ≥0.97 availability with acceptable confidence interval.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of FR-AVAIL-* to NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-FRs (FR-AVAIL-001 through FR-AVAIL-005) are allocated to specific modules: FR-AVAIL-001 (fault detection) to Vehicle and Safety Services, FR-AVAIL-002 (safety vs. failure distinction) to Safety Services, FR-AVAIL-003 (reporting) to NGHE Solution, FR-AVAIL-004 (predictive maintenance) to NGHE Solution, FR-AVAIL-005 (redundancy) to Vehicle and Safety Services. Clear ownership boundaries and interface contracts are required.

recommended_revisions: Refine the telemetry schema to explicitly include all data required for availability monitoring and predictive maintenance (e.g., system state, fault codes, component health metrics, maintenance history, safety status).
Define the predictive maintenance algorithm in detail, including: (1) input data sources, (2) decision logic, (3) maintenance recommendations, (4) confidence thresholds, (5) escalation procedures.

Define the fault recovery procedure in detail, including: (1) fault detection logic, (2) recovery actions (e.g., restart, failover, graceful degradation), (3) success criteria, (4) escalation to human operators.
Establish a clear interface contract between NGHE Solution and subordinate modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) for availability-related data exchange and coordination.
Perform component-level reliability analysis to determine if 0.97 availability is achievable with current architecture, or if additional redundancy/failover mechanisms are required.

decision.justification: The refined FR (CR-NGHE-004) is not atomic; it decomposes into five sub-FRs (FR-AVAIL-001 through FR-AVAIL-005) that address distinct aspects of availability: fault detection, safety vs. failure distinction, reporting, predictive maintenance, and redundancy. Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to define specific design parameters and implementation modules. The selected DP (centralized monitoring + predictive maintenance) is sound at the system level, but requires detailed design at the next level to specify algorithms, interfaces, and component-level responsibilities. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is appropriate, but subordinate modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) must be refined to address their contributions to availability (fault detection, local recovery, redundancy). Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to achieve sufficient detail for implementation.
decision.justification_detail: Gate review confirms that FR-DP-IM alignment is sound, but the level of abstraction is too high for implementation. Sub-FRs require explicit DPs and IMs. For example, FR-AVAIL-001 (fault detection) requires a DP specifying the fault detection algorithm and an IM assigning responsibility to Vehicle and Safety Services modules. FR-AVAIL-004 (predictive maintenance) requires a DP specifying the maintenance algorithm and an IM assigning responsibility to NGHE Solution with coordination across Vehicle and Safety Services. Proceeding to the next level will provide the necessary detail.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-AVAIL-001: The system shall detect and log all unplanned downtime events (failures, faults) with timestamp and root cause.
FR-AVAIL-002: The system shall distinguish between safety-driven unavailability and failure-driven unavailability in availability calculations.
FR-AVAIL-003: The system shall provide real-time availability status and historical availability metrics to the Site Manager and Project Manager.
FR-AVAIL-004: The system shall implement predictive maintenance and fault recovery mechanisms to minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-AVAIL-005: The system shall maintain redundancy or failover capability for critical subsystems to sustain availability targets.

assumptions: Telemetry interface provides sufficient granularity and frequency to detect downtime events within acceptable latency (<1 second).
Predictive maintenance algorithms can be trained on historical failure data; if data is sparse, prediction accuracy may be low.
Availability target of 0.97 is achievable with current architecture; component-level reliability analysis is required to confirm.
Safety-driven unavailability is acceptable and must be distinguished from failure-driven unavailability in availability calculations.
Subordinate modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) can provide the telemetry and fault recovery capabilities required by NGHE Solution.
open_risks: If monitoring system itself fails, availability metrics become unreliable; mitigation requires redundant monitoring or independent verification.
Predictive maintenance may introduce false positives (unnecessary maintenance) or false negatives (missed failures); requires continuous tuning.
Availability target of 0.97 may be unachievable if critical subsystems have inherent reliability <0.97; requires component-level reliability analysis.
Predictive maintenance scheduler may conflict with Vehicle or Safety Services operational constraints; requires clear prioritization rules and coordination protocols.
Telemetry interface may not provide sufficient granularity for accurate availability calculation; requires explicit telemetry requirements and validation.
evidence_needed: Historical failure data for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to train predictive maintenance algorithms.
Component-level reliability specifications (MTBF, MTTF) to assess feasibility of 0.97 availability target.
Telemetry system specifications (data types, frequency, latency) to confirm capability for availability monitoring.
Fault recovery procedure specifications (detection logic, recovery actions, success criteria) for Vehicle and Safety Services modules.
Redundancy and failover architecture specifications for critical subsystems (propulsion, control, safety).
Acceptance criteria and evaluation period definition (e.g., 30 days, 90 days, 1 year) for availability measurement.
